name: Bayesian Cable Curl
resistance_profile: Facing away from a low pulley draws the working arm behind the torso, loading the biceps hard in the deep stretched position while the cable keeps constant tension through the full range.
cues: Let the arm travel behind your body at the bottom
Keep the upper arm still — only the forearm moves
Control the stretch, do not drop into it
execution_steps: Set a cable pulley to its lowest position and attach a single handle.
Grab the handle with an underhand grip and step forward so you face away from the machine with the cable running behind you.
Stagger your stance for balance and let the working arm draw back and straight, feeling a deep stretch in the biceps.
Keeping the upper arm fixed, curl the handle forward and up toward your shoulder.
Squeeze at the top, then slowly let the arm travel back behind the body into the stretch.
good_when: You want maximal loaded stretch on the biceps
You want constant cable tension through the whole range
Isolating one arm at a time with a strong mind-muscle focus
bad_when: No low pulley or cable station is available
You want a heavy bilateral mass builder
common_errors: Letting the elbow drift forward, which kills the stretch
Using the shoulder or torso to swing the weight up
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
regressions: Use a standing front cable curl instead
